title: Algebra tiles / integer chips extension
context: Algebra tiles (positive/negative units, x-tiles, x-squared tiles) and integer chips (two-colour counters used for signed arithmetic) extend the manipulative set into middle-school algebra. Their key relationship invariants are that a positive tile and its negative counterpart exactly cancel (zero pair), and that x-tiles are dimensionally consistent with x-squared tiles (the x-tile width matches the x-squared tile side). These invariants must be machine-verified.
objective: Implement generator modules for algebra tiles and integer chips as an extension to the parametric engine, with machine-verified zero-pair and dimensional-consistency invariants.
acceptanceCriteria: Generates positive and negative unit tiles, x-tiles, and x-squared tiles with clear positive/negative visual distinction not relying on colour alone.
Zero-pair invariant verified: positive and negative versions of each tile type are exactly equal in dimensions (and thus would cancel physically when overlaid).
x-tile / x-squared dimensional consistency verified: the x-tile width equals the x-squared tile side within tolerance.
Integer chips variant generates two-colour counters (positive/negative) with non-colour distinction (e.g. + and - labels or different patterns).
All standard variants generated; math-educator review sign-off recorded.
MIT-licensed; all commits DCO-signed; passes pnpm build, pnpm test, and CI gate.
resources: tasks/math-manip-code-002.json -- verification harness (extend for zero-pair and x-consistency checks)
output: Algebra-tiles and integer-chips generator modules in the engine package, with spec files and example output PDFs, in a pull request, MIT-licensed.
